tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

[{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Sync eng/common directory with azure-sdk-tools for PR 15192 (#58315)

* Add CODEOWNERS verification pipeline step and script

Add verify-codeowners.yml pipeline template step and
Test-CodeownersForArtifacts.ps1 script to validate that packages
intended for release have sufficient CODEOWNERS coverage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Review feedback

* Fail closed on missing release status

---------

Co-authored-by: Daniel Jurek <djurek@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Azure SDK Bot","email":"53356347+azure-sdk@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-18T17:12:08Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-18T17:12:08Z"}},"author":{"login":"azure-sdk","id":53356347,"profile_url":"https://github.com/azure-sdk","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/53356347?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[AgentServer] Observability, API surface, and developer experience improvements (#58314)

* Prepare release: Core beta.22, Responses beta.2, Invocations beta.2

Set release date 2026-05-05 for all three AgentServer packages.
Add Invocations changelog entry for Core dependency update.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(Responses): deterministic agent_session_id derivation (B39)

Implement SHA-256-based session ID derivation matching the Python SDK:
- Seed: "{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}" → 63-char hex
- Priority: request payload → env var → deterministic hash → random
- Partition hint extracted from conversation_id or previous_response_id
- Default agent name 'server-default-agent' when null/empty

11 unit tests + 8 protocol tests updated for new format.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: remove Python references from docs and comments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add inbound request logging middleware to Core

Add InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware that logs all incoming HTTP requests
with method, path, status code, duration, correlation headers
(x-request-id, x-ms-client-request-id), and OpenTelemetry trace ID.
Uses LoggerMessage source generation for high-performance structured
logging. Request start logs at Information level; successful completion
at Information; 4xx/5xx responses at Warning. Query strings are
deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add handler-level diagnostic logging to Responses endpoints

Add Info-level structured logs to all Responses API endpoints:
- POST /responses: responseId, streaming, background, store, model,
  conversationId, previousResponseId (moved after ID generation)
- POST /responses (non-streaming): final status and output count
- POST /responses (background): signal handoff with status
- GET /responses: responseId, retrieved status and output count
- POST /responses/{id}/cancel: responseId and result status
- DELETE /responses/{id}: responseId and success
- GET /responses/{id}/input_items: responseId
- Orchestrator: handler type name on invocation

These logs complement the Core inbound middleware (HTTP-level) with
response-specific context for debugging storage deserialization issues,
handler failures, and request tracing across the full pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update release dates to 2026-04-17

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: exception handling in middleware, cache GetConversationId

- InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware: catch exceptions from next() to force
  status 500 in log output instead of misleading 200; re-read traceId in
  finally block to pick up Activity created later in the pipeline.
- ResponseEndpointHandler: cache GetConversationId() result to avoid
  repeated JSON parsing across partition key, logging, and session ID.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add x-agent-session-id response header on all protocol endpoints (§8)

Implement the spec §8 requirement that every protocol endpoint response
MUST include x-agent-session-id as a response header.

Responses protocol:
- Add SessionIdResponseHeaderFilter endpoint filter (outermost, wraps
  exception filter) that reads resolved session ID from HttpContext.Items
  and falls back to FoundryEnvironment.SessionId
- Store resolved session ID in HttpContext.Items from POST (after B39
  resolution), GET, Cancel, and SSE replay handler paths
- 12 new E2E protocol tests covering all endpoints, error paths, and
  env var fallback

Invocations protocol:
- Add x-agent-session-id header to GET /invocations/{id} and
  POST /invocations/{id}/cancel (POST /invocations already had it)
- Add header to GET /invocations/docs/openapi.json with env var fallback
- 2 new endpoint tests + header assertions on existing GET/Cancel tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename ServerUserAgent to ServerVersion and restructure middleware extensions

Rename all ServerUserAgent* types, files, methods, and properties to
ServerVersion* — the header identifies the server version stack, not a
user-agent string.

Type renames:
- ServerUserAgentRegistry → ServerVersionRegistry
- ServerUserAgentMiddleware → ServerVersionMiddleware
- AgentHostBuilder.UserAgentRegistry → VersionRegistry

Extension method restructure:
- AddAgentServerUserAgent() → AddAgentServerVersion() (version only)
- UseAgentServerUserAgent() → UseAgentServerVersion()
- NEW: AddAgentServerLogging() — registers InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware
- NEW: UseAgentServerLogging() — wires InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware

The old AddAgentServerUserAgent() bundled both version and logging
registration which was confusing. Now each concern has its own pair of
Add/Use methods. Tier 1/2 (AgentHostBuilder) lights up both
automatically; Tier 3 users call them independently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update changelogs for session ID header and ServerVersion rename

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add inbound request logging entries to Responses and Invocations changelogs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move inbound logging to Features Added in changelogs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Internalize framework-only telemetry types

Make AgentHostTelemetry, InvocationsActivitySource, ResponsesActivitySource,
and ResponsesTracingConstants internal. These types are framework plumbing
not needed by handler authors. Reduces public API surface by 128 lines.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Populate session ID in HttpContext.Items for all endpoint paths

SSE replay (in-flight), Delete, and InputItems now store the resolved
session ID so SessionIdResponseHeaderFilter uses the actual response's
AgentSessionId instead of the FoundryEnvironment fallback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add session ID header assertions to Delete and InputItems tests

Both tests now set FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID, assert the header is
present, and verify its value — matching the Cancel test pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Log isolation key presence on endpoint handlers and storage requests

All Responses endpoint handler logs now include HasUserIsolationKey and
HasChatIsolationKey booleans (values are never logged).

Invocations POST, GET, and Cancel handlers now log the same presence
flags alongside the invocation ID.

FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy success/failure logs now include whether
isolation headers were sent on the outbound storage request.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix doc deviations from implementation

- Replace 'user-agent header' with 'server version header' in READMEs,
  AGENTS.md, and MigrationGuide
- Remove stale ResponsesActivitySource subclassing guidance from handler
  guide (type is now internal)
- Update Core README telemetry section (AgentHostTelemetry is internal)
- Add Tier 3 logging extensions to Core README troubleshooting
- Add isolation key presence logging entries to Responses and Invocations
  changelogs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add startup configuration logging across all packages

Core (AgentHostBuilder.Build):
- Logs platform environment: IsHosted, AgentName, AgentVersion, Port,
  SessionId, SseKeepAliveInterval
- Logs connectivity: ProjectEndpoint (masked to scheme://host),
  OtlpEndpoint (masked), AppInsightsConfigured (bool only)
- Logs host options: ShutdownTimeout, registered protocols

Responses (ResponsesStartupLogger IHostedService):
- Logs StorageProvider (FoundryStorage vs InMemory), DefaultModel,
  DefaultFetchHistoryCount, EventStreamTtl

Invocations (InvocationsStartupLogger IHostedService):
- Logs protocol registration

Sensitive values (connection strings, full URIs) are masked or reduced
to boolean presence indicators.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Resolve actual storage provider type for startup log

Use the DI-resolved ResponsesProvider instance's type name instead of
assuming FoundryStorage vs InMemory based on IsHosted. Developers can
register custom providers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve Invocations README: InvocationContext properties, Tier 2 setup

Address documentation gaps from #58190:
- Expand InvocationContext section with full property table (InvocationId,
  SessionId, ClientHeaders, QueryParameters, Isolation)
- Add Tier 2 manual setup example (AgentHost.CreateBuilder) to Invocations README
- Document InvocationsServerOptions in Customizing the host section
- Update Responses README manual setup from Tier 3 to Tier 2 for consistency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Mask sensitive portions of Foundry storage URLs in logs

Redact everything before /storage (scheme, host, project path) and strip
query parameters except api-version. This prevents leaking account and
project information in diagnostic logs.

Example: https://acct.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/myproj/storage/responses/resp_123?api-version=2025-01-01&token=[REDACTED] ***/storage/responses/resp_123?api-version=2025-01-01

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add server version User-Agent to Foundry storage requests

Wire ServerVersionPolicy into the storage HTTP pipeline to prepend
the AgentServer identity string (e.g., azure-ai-agentserver-responses/
1.0.0-beta.2 (dotnet/10.0)) to the User-Agent header on all outbound
Foundry storage API calls.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use shared ServerVersionRegistry for storage User-Agent

Resolve the ServerVersionRegistry from DI instead of building a
standalone identity string. This ensures the storage pipeline
User-Agent includes all registered segments — protocol packages
and any developer-registered custom segments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Read ServerVersionRegistry lazily per-request in storage pipeline

The policy now holds a reference to the registry and reads segments
on each request, so registrations that happen after pipeline
construction (e.g., during endpoint mapping or at runtime) are
always reflected in the outbound User-Agent header.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Responses changelog for storage URL masking and User-Agent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: store session ID on ResponseExecution at creation time

- Add AgentSessionId property to ResponseExecution, set during CreateResponseAsync
- SSE replay and Delete in-flight paths now use execution.AgentSessionId instead
  of execution.Response?.AgentSessionId (which is null before response.created)
- Clarify Invocations README: agent_session_id query param only used on POST

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

* WIP - still does not compile the storage test base cause of double ref to UriQueryParamsCollection

* Fixed last commit; PR feedback

* Add back validation but in one spot for URL snapshot/version check against options

* Remove unnecessary double sanitization

* Fix unit tests to setup Uri that was missing

* Added fix for ensuring snapshot will apply to share directories from options bag

* Cleanup in RehydrateShareResourceTests

* Generate API

* Consolidated checks for valid snapshot/version on uri/options; added missing checks on ShareFiles

* Clearer set snapshot/version error","author":{"name":"Amanda Nguyen","email":"48961492+amnguye@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-04-17T23:09:37Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-17T23:09:37Z"}},"author":{"login":"amnguye","id":48961492,"profile_url":"https://github.com/amnguye","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/48961492?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}}

{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[AgentServer] Observability, API surface, and developer experience improvements (#58314)

* Prepare release: Core beta.22, Responses beta.2, Invocations beta.2

Set release date 2026-05-05 for all three AgentServer packages.
Add Invocations changelog entry for Core dependency update.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(Responses): deterministic agent_session_id derivation (B39)

Implement SHA-256-based session ID derivation matching the Python SDK:
- Seed: "{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}" → 63-char hex
- Priority: request payload → env var → deterministic hash → random
- Partition hint extracted from conversation_id or previous_response_id
- Default agent name 'server-default-agent' when null/empty

11 unit tests + 8 protocol tests updated for new format.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: remove Python references from docs and comments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add inbound request logging middleware to Core

Add InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware that logs all incoming HTTP requests
with method, path, status code, duration, correlation headers
(x-request-id, x-ms-client-request-id), and OpenTelemetry trace ID.
Uses LoggerMessage source generation for high-performance structured
logging. Request start logs at Information level; successful completion
at Information; 4xx/5xx responses at Warning. Query strings are
deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add handler-level diagnostic logging to Responses endpoints

Add Info-level structured logs to all Responses API endpoints:
- POST /responses: responseId, streaming, background, store, model,
  conversationId, previousResponseId (moved after ID generation)
- POST /responses (non-streaming): final status and output count
- POST /responses (background): signal handoff with status
- GET /responses: responseId, retrieved status and output count
- POST /responses/{id}/cancel: responseId and result status
- DELETE /responses/{id}: responseId and success
- GET /responses/{id}/input_items: responseId
- Orchestrator: handler type name on invocation

These logs complement the Core inbound middleware (HTTP-level) with
response-specific context for debugging storage deserialization issues,
handler failures, and request tracing across the full pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update release dates to 2026-04-17

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: exception handling in middleware, cache GetConversationId

- InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware: catch exceptions from next() to force
  status 500 in log output instead of misleading 200; re-read traceId in
  finally block to pick up Activity created later in the pipeline.
- ResponseEndpointHandler: cache GetConversationId() result to avoid
  repeated JSON parsing across partition key, logging, and session ID.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add x-agent-session-id response header on all protocol endpoints (§8)

Implement the spec §8 requirement that every protocol endpoint response
MUST include x-agent-session-id as a response header.

Responses protocol:
- Add SessionIdResponseHeaderFilter endpoint filter (outermost, wraps
  exception filter) that reads resolved session ID from HttpContext.Items
  and falls back to FoundryEnvironment.SessionId
- Store resolved session ID in HttpContext.Items from POST (after B39
  resolution), GET, Cancel, and SSE replay handler paths
- 12 new E2E protocol tests covering all endpoints, error paths, and
  env var fallback

Invocations protocol:
- Add x-agent-session-id header to GET /invocations/{id} and
  POST /invocations/{id}/cancel (POST /invocations already had it)
- Add header to GET /invocations/docs/openapi.json with env var fallback
- 2 new endpoint tests + header assertions on existing GET/Cancel tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename ServerUserAgent to ServerVersion and restructure middleware extensions

Rename all ServerUserAgent* types, files, methods, and properties to
ServerVersion* — the header identifies the server version stack, not a
user-agent string.

Type renames:
- ServerUserAgentRegistry → ServerVersionRegistry
- ServerUserAgentMiddleware → ServerVersionMiddleware
- AgentHostBuilder.UserAgentRegistry → VersionRegistry

Extension method restructure:
- AddAgentServerUserAgent() → AddAgentServerVersion() (version only)
- UseAgentServerUserAgent() → UseAgentServerVersion()
- NEW: AddAgentServerLogging() — registers InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware
- NEW: UseAgentServerLogging() — wires InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware

The old AddAgentServerUserAgent() bundled both version and logging
registration which was confusing. Now each concern has its own pair of
Add/Use methods. Tier 1/2 (AgentHostBuilder) lights up both
automatically; Tier 3 users call them independently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update changelogs for session ID header and ServerVersion rename

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add inbound request logging entries to Responses and Invocations changelogs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move inbound logging to Features Added in changelogs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Internalize framework-only telemetry types

Make AgentHostTelemetry, InvocationsActivitySource, ResponsesActivitySource,
and ResponsesTracingConstants internal. These types are framework plumbing
not needed by handler authors. Reduces public API surface by 128 lines.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Populate session ID in HttpContext.Items for all endpoint paths

SSE replay (in-flight), Delete, and InputItems now store the resolved
session ID so SessionIdResponseHeaderFilter uses the actual response's
AgentSessionId instead of the FoundryEnvironment fallback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add session ID header assertions to Delete and InputItems tests

Both tests now set FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID, assert the header is
present, and verify its value — matching the Cancel test pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Log isolation key presence on endpoint handlers and storage requests

All Responses endpoint handler logs now include HasUserIsolationKey and
HasChatIsolationKey booleans (values are never logged).

Invocations POST, GET, and Cancel handlers now log the same presence
flags alongside the invocation ID.

FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy success/failure logs now include whether
isolation headers were sent on the outbound storage request.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix doc deviations from implementation

- Replace 'user-agent header' with 'server version header' in READMEs,
  AGENTS.md, and MigrationGuide
- Remove stale ResponsesActivitySource subclassing guidance from handler
  guide (type is now internal)
- Update Core README telemetry section (AgentHostTelemetry is internal)
- Add Tier 3 logging extensions to Core README troubleshooting
- Add isolation key presence logging entries to Responses and Invocations
  changelogs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add startup configuration logging across all packages

Core (AgentHostBuilder.Build):
- Logs platform environment: IsHosted, AgentName, AgentVersion, Port,
  SessionId, SseKeepAliveInterval
- Logs connectivity: ProjectEndpoint (masked to scheme://host),
  OtlpEndpoint (masked), AppInsightsConfigured (bool only)
- Logs host options: ShutdownTimeout, registered protocols

Responses (ResponsesStartupLogger IHostedService):
- Logs StorageProvider (FoundryStorage vs InMemory), DefaultModel,
  DefaultFetchHistoryCount, EventStreamTtl

Invocations (InvocationsStartupLogger IHostedService):
- Logs protocol registration

Sensitive values (connection strings, full URIs) are masked or reduced
to boolean presence indicators.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Resolve actual storage provider type for startup log

Use the DI-resolved ResponsesProvider instance's type name instead of
assuming FoundryStorage vs InMemory based on IsHosted. Developers can
register custom providers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve Invocations README: InvocationContext properties, Tier 2 setup

Address documentation gaps from #58190:
- Expand InvocationContext section with full property table (InvocationId,
  SessionId, ClientHeaders, QueryParameters, Isolation)
- Add Tier 2 manual setup example (AgentHost.CreateBuilder) to Invocations README
- Document InvocationsServerOptions in Customizing the host section
- Update Responses README manual setup from Tier 3 to Tier 2 for consistency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Mask sensitive portions of Foundry storage URLs in logs

Redact everything before /storage (scheme, host, project path) and strip
query parameters except api-version. This prevents leaking account and
project information in diagnostic logs.

Example: https://acct.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/myproj/storage/responses/resp_123?api-version=2025-01-01&token=[REDACTED] ***/storage/responses/resp_123?api-version=2025-01-01

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add server version User-Agent to Foundry storage requests

Wire ServerVersionPolicy into the storage HTTP pipeline to prepend
the AgentServer identity string (e.g., azure-ai-agentserver-responses/
1.0.0-beta.2 (dotnet/10.0)) to the User-Agent header on all outbound
Foundry storage API calls.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use shared ServerVersionRegistry for storage User-Agent

Resolve the ServerVersionRegistry from DI instead of building a
standalone identity string. This ensures the storage pipeline
User-Agent includes all registered segments — protocol packages
and any developer-registered custom segments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Read ServerVersionRegistry lazily per-request in storage pipeline

The policy now holds a reference to the registry and reads segments
on each request, so registrations that happen after pipeline
construction (e.g., during endpoint mapping or at runtime) are
always reflected in the outbound User-Agent header.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Responses changelog for storage URL masking and User-Agent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: store session ID on ResponseExecution at creation time

- Add AgentSessionId property to ResponseExecution, set during CreateResponseAsync
- SSE replay and Delete in-flight paths now use execution.AgentSessionId instead
  of execution.Response?.AgentSessionId (which is null before response.created)
- Clarify Invocations README: agent_session_id query param only used on POST

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Ravi Pidaparthi","email":"rapida@microsoft.com","date":"2026-04-18T09:16:11Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-04-18T09:16:11Z"}},"author":{"login":"RaviPidaparthi","id":28370364,"profile_url":"https://github.com/RaviPidaparthi","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/28370364?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}}

arguments.title: [Storage] Docs: Document snapshot and versioning transfer support in Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs README
